patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 1 dominates the slate — 7 of 11 scorable non-D games carry negative edge, preventing any GOLD+ stake; confirmed non-negotiable even on pre-cap A-/B+ stacks per 6/21 and 7/7 postgame archive.

D-grade dual-opposing-signal pattern (SP xERA against + PQI against) fires on 7 matchups today (NYY@WSH, SEA@TB, HOU@TEX, CHC@CIN, ATL@STL, TOR@SD, OAK@CHW); per the 7/8-7/9 archive, dual simultaneous opposing signals treat the pick direction as noise, not a bet.

F1_xera_gap* (starred thin-sample SP) recurs on MIL@PIT G1/G2 (Ashcraft/Sproat, Chandler/Drohan) and ARI@LAD (Yamamoto); even with F2/F3 corroboration, starred flag combined with negative market edge cannot be rescued per the 7/9 rule.

ARI@LAD carries F3=13767.8 (largest swing-take gap observed this season) yet market prices LAD at -19.44pp edge — Yamamoto is 0-for-2 in recent tracked ARI starts (6/10, 7/10); market has updated on live Yamamoto form faster than the xERA feature.

MIL @ PIT G1.reasoning: All four grading dimensions fire (F1*=1.12+F3=1545.9+F5=+0.75, SP 0.79 agrees, PQI Δ=-14.7, Stage agree), raw pre-cap score=5. However, HARD CAP 1 (-2.05pp) is non-negotiable per the 6/21 and 7/7 archive — even elite triple-F stacks with small negative edges were confirmed as proper SKIPs. F1* is starred (thin Ashcraft sample) and F3=1545.9 is at the low end of meaningful. confidence_downgrade=True and BvP all NO_DATA/SMALL_SAMPLE. Makeup doubleheader context adds scheduling risk. SKIP endorsed.

MIL @ PIT G2.reasoning: Same signal stack as G1 (F1*=1.12, F3=1545.9, F5=+0.75, all four dimensions fire at raw pre-cap=5). G2 SP pairing is Chandler vs Drohan — both with even thinner Statcast samples than G1, making F1*=1.12 less trustworthy. HARD CAP 1 (-2.05pp) confirmed non-negotiable. Home lineup concentration=1.002 (PIT perfectly balanced) does not add rescue signal. Doubleheader fatigue compounds uncertainty. SKIP endorsed.

LAA @ MIN.reasoning: Only F3=1766.4 fires (single signal, no F1 or F2). Stage 1/2 delta=0.2241 (Stage 1 gives LAA ~79% F5 probability). Per the 6/20 lesson, f5d≥0.18 combined with only F3 is insufficient for Stage-2 rescue — Stage 1's large LAA lean is a credible directional warning. confidence_downgrade=True and bullpen_fatigue compound uncertainty. The negative edge (-18.43pp) also triggers CAP 1. SKIP confirmed.

OAK @ CHW.reasoning: F3=3353.3 fires but SP edge 0.66 xERA AGAINST CHW and PQI AGAINST (Δ=-10.3) produce grade=D, score=-1. Two independent opposing signals outweigh the single F3 signal. Per 7/8-7/9 archive, D-grade picks with SP against + PQI against are directional noise (~55% of outcomes favor the opposing team). The stale GOLD tier label does not change the D-grade reality. BvP all NO_DATA. SKIP and low confidence in CHW direction confirmed.

COL @ SF.reasoning: Strong signal stack: F1=2.03 (not starred), F5=+2.04, SP 1.64 agrees, PQI +20.8, Stage agree — raw pre-cap=5. SF home batters show 2 OWNER BvP signals vs Freeland (Ramos 16PA shrunk 0.861, Schmitt 10PA shrunk 0.890). Despite these qualitative strengths, HARD CAP 1 (-2.88pp) is non-negotiable; 7/7 BOS@CWS confirmed even small negative edges block elite triple-F stacks on long-run EV grounds. F3=125.5 is extremely weak. confidence_downgrade=True and acute_roster flag present. OWNER BvP noted but insufficient to rescue against CAP 1.

NYY @ WSH.reasoning: SP gap 2.02 xERA against WSH (Schlittler NYY >> Poulin WSH) plus PQI strongly against (-34.6) overwhelm F3=4955 and F5_bullpen=+1.64 positive signals. Market prices WSH fair_prob=38.8% (NYY implied ~61.2%), heavily confirming the opposing SP edge. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.048 (WSH bullpen meaningfully better) and F3=4955 provide some directional support for WSH but are insufficient to overcome a 2.02 SP gap and -34.6 PQI. confidence_downgrade=True and short_term_skill flag further suppress. D-grade and WSH direction appear suspect; no OVERRIDE due to absent dual-OWNER BvP for NYY.

BOS @ NYM.reasoning: Eduardo Rivera (NYM home SP) has only 17 Statcast pitches season-to-date against the 100-pitch minimum required for xERA scoring. No SP edge can be scored; the pipeline correctly withholds the pick. No directional evaluation is possible without scored SP data. Process correct; hard stop confirmed.

SEA @ TB.reasoning: SP 1.48 xERA AGAINST TB (Logan Gilbert SEA > Griffin Jax TB) plus PQI AGAINST (-19.0) produce grade=D. Stage 1/2 delta=0.2421 means Stage 1 gives SEA ~60% F5 probability — a structural warning that the full-game TB call depends on late leverage the model may not reliably capture. SEA batters show elevated BvP vs Jax: Arozarena (7PA shrunk 0.881), Raleigh (8PA shrunk 1.021, near OWNER threshold). These BvP signals reinforce SEA's advantage. D-grade SKIP confirmed; TB direction appears structurally weak.

CLE @ MIA.reasoning: No F-signal fires (signals column empty); pre-cap A- (score=4) is built on SP edge 0.61 + PQI +11.1 + Stage agree only — three dimensions, not four. The CAP-10 rescue requires all four dimensions including F-signal; without it, rescue is disqualified regardless of pre-cap level. HARD CAP 1 (-3.36pp) plus CAP 10 plus confidence_downgrade=True plus bullpen_fatigue all suppress. Yesterday CLE won 3-2 in this matchup as the model pick; today's MIA reversal without F-signal backing warrants extra caution. SKIP confirmed.

PHI @ DET.reasoning: F3=328.8 barely fires (well below the 1000 meaningful threshold), PQI against DET (-4.6), and CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.540. PHI brings an elite lineup (Turner, Schwarber, Harper, Marsh) against Casey Mize; PHI batters' BvP vs Mize is thin SMALL_SAMPLE with neutral shrunk OPS. Pre-cap B- (score=1) with a weak single F-signal does not clear the rescue bar. No SP edge anchor (SP signals absent from grade_reasons). SKIP confirmed.

KC @ BAL.reasoning: F1=1.35 (not starred) plus F3=3325.5, SP 1.02 agrees, PQI +19.7 confirm — a solid B+ stack. However, pre-cap B+ (score=3) falls below the pre-cap A threshold needed for CAP-10 rescue. HARD CAP 1 (-9.65pp) is the primary suppressor; the 7/10 postgame confirmed this identical matchup as a proper SKIP when BAL last faced KC. Market has absorbed Bradish's value at -9.65pp. bullpen_fatigue flag noted. SKIP confirmed.

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: SP 1.04 xERA AGAINST TEX (Rocker HOU > Lambert TEX) plus PQI AGAINST (-18.1) plus confidence_downgrade=True produce D-grade. TEX home batters show Joc Pederson as a single OWNER vs Lambert (19PA shrunk OPS 0.929), but OVERRIDE requires dual-OWNER signals plus SP gap favoring TEX — here SP runs against TEX. Edge is essentially flat (-0.23pp). One OWNER cannot flip D-grade with two opposing signals. SKIP and low confidence in TEX direction confirmed.

CHC @ CIN.reasoning: SP 1.47 xERA AGAINST CIN (Assad CHC > Lodolo CIN) plus PQI AGAINST (-18.5) plus negative team_quality modifier — three dimensions opposing. CIN home batters vs Assad show De La Cruz MEANINGFUL (13PA shrunk 0.772) and Steer MEANINGFUL (20PA shrunk 0.646, near WEAK_VS territory) — no OWNER for CIN. CHC batters have zero BvP history vs Lodolo, preventing an OVERRIDE to CHC despite Assad's quality advantage. Market barely prices CIN at 50.6% (coin-flip). D-grade SKIP confirmed.

ATL @ STL.reasoning: SP 1.00 xERA AGAINST STL (López ATL > Liberatore STL) plus PQI strongly against (-23.3). All batter and BvP data empty, preventing any OVERRIDE anchor. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0175 gives STL a slight bullpen edge, the lone positive signal. Without BvP data or dual-OWNER signals, OVERRIDE to ATL is unwarranted. D-grade SKIP confirmed.

TOR @ SD.reasoning: SP 1.36 xERA AGAINST SD (Yesavage TOR > Buehler SD — Buehler's injury-related regression suppresses his xERA) plus PQI AGAINST (-21.2) plus team_quality_mod negative, confidence_downgrade=True, acute_roster flag. All batter and BvP data empty. Market prices SD at only 50.6% (near coin-flip) while model gives SD 62.6%. Without BvP data for TOR vs Buehler, an OVERRIDE to TOR lacks the dual-OWNER anchor required. D-grade SKIP confirmed; SD direction appears structurally suspect.

ARI @ LAD.reasoning: F3=13767.8 (extraordinary, largest on slate), F2=0.040, F1*=1.67* (starred — thin Yamamoto live-season sample), SP 1.12 agrees, PQI +19.6. Despite the compelling F-signal stack, HARD CAP 1 at -19.44pp is decisive: the market has priced LAD well past the model's claimed edge. Yamamoto is 0-for-2 in recent tracked ARI starts (7/10 lost 9-3, 6/10 tracked loss) — the market appears to have updated on his live form faster than xERA. Stage 1 favors ARI in F5 (f5_prob=0.3961 for home team). Pre-cap B+ (score=3) falls below the A threshold for CAP-10 rescue. F3=13767.8 flagged for the 30-pick monitoring window.
